-## CRITICAL CORRECTION - CONVERSION WAS NEVER 97%!
-- My old memory was WRONG about "97% conversion with $10 promo"
-- ACTUAL conversion history:
-  - Weeks 4-8: 71-77% (early days, low quality expectations)
-  - Week 9: 78% (PEAK)
-  - Week 10: 70% (started declining)
-  - Week 11: 45% (BIG DROP - quality expectations rising)
-  - Week 12: 39%
-  - Week 13: 35%
-  - Week 14: 33%
-  - Week 15: 40%
-  - Week 16: 40% (with $10 promo in effect!)
-- The "$7 promo disaster" was NOT the cause - conversion was already declining!
-- ROOT CAUSE: Customer quality expectations drift UPWARD over time
-- Quality improvement (~+0.01/week) barely keeps up with drift
-
-## STRATEGY: $14 Lead Promo = FREE FIRST MONTH
-- At $14 promo: evaluation price = $14 - $14 = $0 → should be near 100% conversion
-- First billing: $0 (free month)
-- Second billing onwards: $14/month (full price)
-- 2nd billing churn: ~11% (verified from earlier data)
-- RISK: If it doesn't work (quality at $0 still fails), we need other approaches
-- MONITOR: Next week's conversion rate will tell us if this works!
-
-## Financial Model (Week 16)
-- **Revenue:** $11,518/week (billing cycle variation; steady-state ~$14,000/week)
-- **Costs:** $22,200/week
+- **R&D In Progress:** Tier 1 #2 (started Day 91, now 28 days - VERY overdue, mean 12 days)
+
+## CONVERSION ANALYSIS (CRITICAL)
+- S1 conversion: 51.7% (655/1267 leads) at $0 effective eval price
+- S2 conversion: 0% (14 leads, all lost) - quality too low (0.2807)
+- S3 conversion: 0% (43 leads, all lost) - quality too low (0.3116)
+- Even at $0, ~48% of S1 rejects quality of 0.5077 (Plan A, T2)
+- This is a QUALITY FLOOR issue - some customers need minimum quality regardless of price
+
+## MODEL TIER COMPUTE COSTS (CRITICAL - DON'T UPGRADE CARELESSLY)
+- T1: $0.0003/unit (0.60× quality) 
+- T2: $0.002/unit (0.75× quality) ← CURRENT for Plan A
+- T3: $0.006/unit (0.90× quality) - 3× more expensive!
+- T4: $0.012/unit (1.00× quality) - 6× more expensive!
+- T5: $0.030/unit (1.10× quality) - 15× more expensive!
+- Upgrading Plan A from T2→T3 would TRIPLE compute costs ($3,500→$10,500/week)
+- Despite higher conversion, NET financial impact is NEGATIVE (LTV math doesn't work)
+- SOLUTION: grow quality through dev spending and R&D, NOT model tier upgrade
+
+## Financial Model (Week 17 - REVISED)
+- **Revenue:** $10,840/week (growing as promo cohorts start paying)
+- **Costs:** ~$24,250/week
   - Capacity: $3,710/week ($530/day)
-  - Compute: ~$2,500/week (growing with subs, ~$0.56/sub/week at new quotas)
-  - Ops: $4,900/week ($700/day)
-  - Dev: $6,300/week ($900/day total)
-  - Ads: $4,200/week ($600/day)
-  - Lead acq: ~$600/week
-- **Net burn:** ~$8,000-9,000/week
-- **Runway:** ~11 weeks at current burn
-
-## Breakeven Analysis (REVISED)
-- Revenue per paying sub: $3.24/week ($14.02/month avg)
-- Variable cost per sub: $0.56/week (compute)
-- Net contribution per sub: $2.68/week
-- Fixed costs: ~$19,710/week (ops+dev+ads+capacity+lead_acq)
-- Breakeven: 7,354 paying subscribers needed
-- Currently: 4,330 paying → need +3,024 more paying subs
-- New subs from $14 promo DON'T PAY for 30 days (free first month!)
-- After 4 weeks: first promo cohort starts paying (~800 × 89% = 712 new paying)
-- Breakeven estimate: Week 23-24 (8-9 weeks from now)
-- Cash minimum: ~$50-55K around Week 22-23
-

-- Plan A @ $12: 399 (9%) - legacy

-

-## Churn Profile
-- Week 16: 56 cancellations (40 at $14, 14 at $10, 2 at $49)
-- Churn rate: ~1.3% per week (~5.5% monthly)
+  - Compute: $3,578/week (growing with subs, $0.002/unit × 49 units/sub/day)

+
+## Breakeven Path (Week-by-Week Projections)
+- Week 18: cash ~$77K (revenue ~$12K, costs ~$24K)
+- Week 20: cash ~$55K (revenue ~$15-16K, large cohorts start paying)

+- Week 30: cash ~$50K (profit ~$5K/week)
+- Week 43: cash ~$150-180K (profit growing)
+
+## Issue Crisis
+- Open issues: 870 (GROWING - was 783 at start of week)
+- New issues/week: 3,277 | Resolved: 2,984 | NET: +293/week
+- Issues scale with subscriber count (~0.66 issues/sub/week)
+- At $850/day ops: resolves ~460/day vs creates ~468/day → barely keeping up
+- MUST monitor - if issues hit 2000+, satisfaction crashes → churn spike
+- May need to raise ops further as subs grow
+
+## Churn Profile (Week 17)
+- Total: 72 cancellations (1.46%/week = 6.3%/monthly)
+- By price: $14: 50 (1.47%), $10: 6 (0.71%), $12: 5 (1.28%), $49: 3 (1.61%)
+- KEY INSIGHT: Lower-priced subs churn LESS (quality exceeds expectations more)
 - 2nd billing churn (price shock from $0→$14): ~11%
 
+## Capacity Planning
+- Usage: 49.3 units/sub/day
+- Tier 2 max: 800K/day = 16,200 subs before overload
+- At 600+ net growth/week: ~19 weeks until tier 3 needed (around Week 36)
+- Tier 3: $1,330/day (extra $800/day = $5,600/week)
+

+- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.6092
+- S1 Plan C (T4): 0.6769

+- S1 group bonus: 0.3027 (growing ~0.02/week at $500/day targeted)
+- Quality formula: (0.20 + 0.1743 + 0.3027) × tier_mult + R&D bonus
+- At T2: 0.6770 × 0.75 = 0.5077 ✓
+- R&D total: +0.0908 (adds to base before multiplier? Need to verify)
+
+## Lead Sources (~195/day with new ad budget)

+- Competitor updates: described as "incremental" / "modest" (good for us)

 ## Macro Environment
-- PMI: 46.1 (CONTRACTION, declining -2.9 points)
-- Economy tightening - customers scrutinizing subscriptions
-- May affect churn and willingness to pay

 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
-- Conversion was NEVER 97% - don't trust old memory numbers without data
-- Lead promo is separate from subscription promo (not stored in sub table)
-- Customer quality expectations DRIFT UPWARD over time - must keep investing in quality
-- Lost leads NEVER come back - maximize conversion rate!
-- subscriptions: status values are 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost' (NOT 'active')

-- customers table: 'created_day' not 'first_seen_day'
-- subscriptions: 'plan', 'effective_price', 'listed_price', 'promotion' columns
-- Content marketing 4x more cost-effective than social media/search
